meta.title: End Behavior of f(x) = 2x³ + x − 2 | MAT 170 Exam 2

steps.content: Determine the end behavior of:  f(x) = 2x³ + x − 2
steps.narration: Determine the end behavior of f of x equals 2 x cubed plus x minus 2.
steps.label: MAT 170 Exam 2 Review

steps.narration: Here is our function: 2 x cubed plus x minus 2.

steps.content: Leading Coefficient Test: The end behavior of a polynomial is determined ONLY by its leading term (the term with the highest power of x).
steps.narration: We use the Leading Coefficient Test. End behavior depends only on the leading term.
steps.label: Leading Coefficient Test

steps.narration: The leading term is 2 x cubed.
The degree is 3, which is odd.
The leading coefficient is 2, which is positive.

steps.content:
Odd degree + Positive leading coefficient:
• As x → −∞, f(x) → −∞  (falls to the left)
• As x → +∞, f(x) → +∞  (rises to the right)
steps.narration: The pattern for odd degree with a positive leading coefficient: the graph falls to the left and rises to the right. This is the standard cubic shape.
steps.label: End Behavior Pattern

steps.narration: As x goes to negative infinity, f of x goes to negative infinity. The graph falls on the left.
As x goes to positive infinity, f of x goes to positive infinity. The graph rises on the right.

steps.content:
End behavior:  Falls left, Rises right
As x → −∞, f(x) → −∞
As x → +∞, f(x) → +∞
steps.narration: The end behavior is: falls to the left and rises to the right.
steps.label: Final Answer

steps.narration: Here is the graph of 2 x cubed plus x minus 2. You can see it falls on the left and rises on the right — the standard behavior for an odd-degree polynomial with a positive leading coefficient.
